hero.title: World Environment Day 2026
hero.ministryName: The Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources of the Republic of Azerbaijan

announcement.title: Republic of Azerbaijan to Host World Environment Day 2026
announcement.description:
World Environment Day 2026 will be hosted in Baku at the Heydar Aliyev Center, in collaboration with the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP). The event will bring together international climate leaders, government officials, partners, and environmental champions.
The programme will combine high-level discussions with public outdoor activities, creating an inclusive national celebration of nature and climate action. The event will take place on June 5 at the Heydar Aliyev Center and will include both closed sessions and an outdoor exhibition open to the public.

aboutHero.title: World Environment Day 2026: A Global Call for Climate Action

aboutEvent.title: About the Event
aboutEvent.description1: World Environment Day 2026 is a large-scale international initiative created to strengthen cooperation in environmental innovation, sustainable development, and green technologies in Azerbaijan and beyond. The event brings together government institutions, startups, technology companies, environmental organizations, students, and international partners to contribute to building a greener and more sustainable future.
aboutEvent.description2: The primary goal of the project is to encourage innovative approaches to environmental challenges, accelerate the adoption of green technologies, and increase youth engagement in sustainability initiatives. The event will feature panel discussions, startup presentations, technology exhibitions, workshops, and networking sessions designed to inspire collaboration and innovation. <0>Click here for more details.</0>
aboutEvent.description3: World Environment Day 2026 is not only an event but also an international platform with a mission to help create a healthier and safer world for future generations. Participants will have the opportunity to establish new partnerships, exchange ideas, and present innovative solutions for environmental issues. The initiative aims to showcase Azerbaijan’s progress in green energy, digital transformation, and environmental sustainability on an international level. At the same time, it creates valuable opportunities for young entrepreneurs and innovators to connect with the global ecosystem and expand their impact. World Environment Day 2026 is a unique platform where innovation, technology, and environmental responsibility come together to shape a better future.

theme1.title: Biodiversity conservation and ecosystem restoration
theme1.description: Azerbaijan is expanding nature protected areas, restoring degraded forests and lands, ecosystems from the Caspian Sea coastline to inland landscapes through afforestation, biodiversity conservation and sustainable environmental management practices to strengthen climate resilience, positioning nature as a key solution to climate change.

theme2.title: Climate action
theme2.description: Azerbaijan aims to balance socio-economic priorities with environmental sustainability, ensuring that climate action efforts across the economy align with the country’s commitments under NDC 3.0 within the framework of the Paris Agreement, while reinforcing its national policy framework to support sustainable development.

theme3.title: Renewable Energy
theme3.description: Azeribaijan is accelerating its transition to renewable energy through large-scale solar and wind projects. Strategic investments aim to reduce emissions, increase energy efficiency, and support clean energy future.

joinMovement.title: Join the movement for nature
joinMovement.description: Join us in shaping World Environment Day 2026 by becoming a partner. Collaborate, contribute, and be part of a global effort to drive climate action and environmental impact.

contactUs.title: Contact Us
contactUs.description: Reach out to us for inquiries, partnerships, or more information about WED 2026. Our team is ready to support your ideas, guide your participation, and collaborate on initiatives that contribute to a more sustainable and resilient future.
